title: hieracium neocerinthe subsp. rhomboidale
intro: Hieracium neocerinthe subsp. rhomboidale, also known as hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health issues. Some of the health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and provide relief from skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Hieracium neocerinthe subsp. rhomboidale are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used to support overall well-being and promote natural healing processes.
